description: Multi-turn conversational eval (B4). Each conversation is replayed against ONE durable server session (authenticated). A turn asserts the resolved operation and that expected_filters are a subset of the actual filters. Conversational invariants: 'expect_inherited' requires context.inherited; 'expect_carried' lists slots that must appear in context.carried; 'expect_clarify' requires the resolver to decline with a clarify prompt; 'forbid_count' lists result.count values that must NEVER appear (the confidently-wrong all-time numbers the feature exists to prevent). forbid_count and expect_clarify are HARD failures regardless of pass-rate threshold. A filter value of '*' means the key must be present and non-empty.

conversations.note: The headline regression: follow-up must inherit count_low_reviews + last_month, NOT become an all-time count_orders (the 6882 failure).

turns.question: How many low reviews did we get last month?
and how many for Rio de Janeiro?

conversations.note: Follow-up swaps the city but must keep the inherited status + date.

turns.question: How many delivered orders did we have in Sao Paulo last month?

conversations.note: Revenue follow-up overlays a state while inheriting the date window.

turns.question: What was our total revenue last month?

conversations.note: A fully-specified new question must NOT inherit the prior turn.

turns.question: What was our total revenue last month?
How many delivered orders did we have this year?

conversations.note: top_products can't filter by city; a 'for <city>' follow-up must clarify, never silently return the national top-N.

turns.question: What are our best selling products?
